name: Parse AZ
func:
msg.payload= msg.payload = parseInt(msg.payload.substr(3,3))

return msg;

name: AZ Input
func:
msg.payload = "<PST><AZIMUTH>"+msg.payload+"</AZIMUTH></PST>"
return msg;

name: AZ Stop
func:
msg.payload = "<PST><STOP>1</STOP></PST>"
return msg;

name: PST RotatorAZ map control
info:
This code is based off of LA9VKA 
rotator code.

Changes
* Moved to dashboard 2.0
* Removed the great SVG beam graphic as 2.0 
  does not have that node yet (I do miss it)
* Added presets dropdown
   You must edit these for your true bearing
   and what you want it to point towards.

name: Poll AZ every 10 sec to update while idle
AZ Input
func:
msg.payload = "<PST><AZIMUTH>"+msg.payload+"</AZIMUTH></PST>"
return msg;

name: Azimuth Map
LA-1K Command
func:
msg.payload = "AR1;\r\n";
return msg;

name: Frequency
func:
msg.payload = msg.payload/1000
return msg;

name: FWD Power
Band
Key Status
Operate
func:
msg.payload = "AM1;\r\n";
return msg;

name: Standby
func:
msg.payload = "AM2;\r\n";
return msg;

name: Mode Config

info:
The msg.payload is set to open or closed and
sent to MQTT so that the Steppir controller
will know to open or close the gate.

When Closed, the Steppir code will not send commands
to the controller and hopefully not cause the 
Steppir to actually move while in TX

The msg.payload is set to open or closed and

to the controller and hopefully not cause the 
Steppir to actually move while in TX

info:
I only use one slice to Send on when remote
So the other slices are still here for your
useage but only the active slice I display
in the dashbaords

This integrates with the Palstar LAâ€‘1K 
amplifier. When the amp is in Operate mode, 
I set the radioâ€™s RF Power to 30 watts. 
When the amp is in Standby,
 I increase RF Power to 90 watts. 
 Adjust these values as needed for
  \your setup.

Also check the RF Power nodes which displays
the radioâ€™s current power setting.
It prevents exceeding 50 watts when the amp 
is in Operate mode. Update the limits to 
match your amplifierâ€™s requirements.

This section displays the radioâ€™s efficiency
 by dividing RF output power by the radioâ€™s 
 electrical input power. This feature is 
 currently only available on the 
 8000â€‘series models because the required 
 powerâ€‘consumption sensor is not present 
 on the 6000â€‘series radios. 
 
 FlexRadio has an open bug report 
 tracking this limitation.

This grouping allows you control APD on/off
from the dashboard and see if it is
calibrating or on/off.  

It seems a little complex and I may update 
this grouping later to simplify. 

But also note, APD seems a little buggy on
my 8600 right now.
